title: Researchers argue that secure agents must treat the model as untrusted
friendlyTitle: Agent safety should not depend on the AI always behaving well
summary: A security research paper argues that stronger models alone cannot make agents safe and that the model should be treated as an untrusted system component. The authors analyse 11 real-world agent attacks and propose enforcing security invariants through permissions, isolation and established systems-security techniques.
friendlySummary: Researchers say an AI model inside an agent should be treated as something that can make mistakes or be tricked. Safety rules should be enforced by the surrounding software.
whyItMatters: The recommendation is directly actionable for agent builders: do not let model confidence define authority. Keep credentials scoped, isolate execution, validate actions outside the model and require human approval for irreversible or high-impact operations.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Give agents only the access they need. Use sandboxes, separate checks and human approval before important or permanent actions.
